display.purpose: Hero headline only, one per page.

title.purpose: Card and case-study titles; brightens to Signal Blue on hover.

label.purpose: Mono uppercase-tracked metadata: eyebrows, client names, categories.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Default easing for all entrance and reveal animations site-wide.

motion.name: duration-reveal
motion.purpose: Scroll-triggered whileInView reveals, optionally staggered by idx * 100ms.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action button. Ink fill, sharp corners, transitions to Signal Blue on hover/focus.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 16px 32px; background-color: #111111; color: #ffffff; font-weight: 500; border-radius: 0; text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 0.3s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover, .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { background-color: #2563eb; outline: none; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #fafafa, 0 0 0 4px #2563eb; }

components.name: Tag
components.description: Static metadata chip used for tech/category tags. Never interactive.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-block; padding: 4px 12px; background-color: #0000000d; color: #111111cc; font-size: 0.75rem; border-radius: 0; }

components.name: Work Card
components.description: Image-and-title project card. No border, no shadow, no radius — image scales on hover, title shifts to Signal Blue.
components.html:
Project Title

Full-stack engineer
components.css: .ds-work-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 24px; text-decoration: none; } .ds-work-card-media { position: relative; aspect-ratio: 4 / 3; overflow: hidden; background-color: #f3f4f6; } .ds-work-card-media img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; transition: transform 1s; } .ds-work-card:hover .ds-work-card-media img { transform: scale(1.05); } .ds-work-card-title { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #111111; transition: color 0.3s; } .ds-work-card:hover .ds-work-card-title { color: #2563eb; } .ds-work-card-role { color: #6b7280; }

components.name: Editorial Split Row
components.description: Signature 12-column layout: narrow metadata rail (client/date) on the left, wide content column on the right. Carries into future list-style pages like /work.
components.html:
CLIENT NAME

6 weeks

Case study title

One or two sentences describing the engagement and outcome.

AstroReact
components.css: .ds-split-row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(12, 1fr); gap: 32px; } .ds-split-meta { grid-column: span 4; } .ds-split-client { font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #6b7280; } .ds-split-date { color: #9ca3af; margin-top: 4px; } .ds-split-content { grid-column: span 8; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 24px; } .ds-split-title { font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #111111; transition: color 0.5s; } .ds-split-row:hover .ds-split-title { color: #2563eb; } .ds-split-desc { font-size: 1.125rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #4b5563; max-width: 60ch; } .ds-split-tags { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; margin-top: 16px; }

narrative.overview:
This site reads like annotations in a lab notebook, not a marketing brochure: monochrome type carrying nearly all the weight, a single utilitarian accent reserved for signal (links, primary actions, hover states), and structure built from whitespace and hairline rules rather than cards or shadows. Nothing is decorated to look impressive — it's arranged to be read quickly and trusted immediately, the same way a well-kept notebook earns trust through legibility, not polish.

The system explicitly rejects the consultant sales-deck register it's evolving away from (stats-grid "proof" sections, "Book a call" CTAs, service-tier offerings), the generic SaaS/AI aesthetic (purple gradients, glassmorphism, hero-metric templates), and the stiff formality of a corporate resume PDF. It is warm through voice and content, not through visual softness — the palette and shapes stay plain so the writing can carry the personality.
keyCharacteristics: Monochrome base (off-white / near-black) with one electric-blue signal color used sparingly
Zero border-radius anywhere — every shape is a sharp rectangle
Flat by default — no shadows at rest; separation comes from whitespace and 1px hairline dividers
Generous vertical rhythm (96–128px between sections) against tight, editorial line lengths
Motion is a confirmation, not a performance: fades and small upward translations, nothing bouncy

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue appears in exactly one state at a time — a hover, a focus ring, a selection highlight. It never sits on the page at rest. If two elements are blue simultaneously, something is wrong.

rules.name: The One Eyebrow Rule
rules.body: Uppercase-tracked mono labels are reserved for genuine metadata (a client name, a status), never used as decorative section kickers. If a section doesn't have real label-shaped data, it gets a plain Headline with no eyebrow above it.

rules.name: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: If it's part of the page's normal layout, it has no shadow and no radius. Shadows are earned only by content that overlays the page (modals, menus, tooltips) — never by a card, button, or section sitting in normal flow.

dos: Do keep every corner sharp — zero border-radius anywhere, no exceptions for cards, buttons, tags, or images.
Do hold Signal Blue (#2563eb) in reserve for exactly one active state at a time — hover, focus, or selection — never at rest.
Do use the 1px Hairline (#0000001a) as the only section-separation device; prefer whitespace over any new visual boundary.
Do cap body text between 25ch and 65ch depending on column width, and keep the mono Label style exclusive to genuine metadata.
Do let motion read as confirmation (fade + small upward translation, ease [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1]) — never bounce, elastic, or overshoot.
donts: Don't reintroduce the consultant sales-deck register — no stats-grid "proof" sections, no "Book a call" CTA, no service-tier offering lists.
Don't use purple/multi-color gradients, glassmorphism, or a hero-metric template (big number + small label + gradient accent) — the generic SaaS/AI look this system explicitly rejects.
Don't let the site read like a stiff, formal resume PDF — copy stays first-person and conversational even where structure is dense.
Don't add a drop shadow or colored border-stripe to any card, button, or list item sitting in normal page flow — reserve shadow for genuinely overlaid surfaces only (modals, dropdowns, tooltips).
Don't apply an uppercase mono eyebrow to every section head as decoration — it's reserved for sections with real label-shaped metadata (client, status, date).
